chapter_title.ar: بَابُ صَلَاةِ الْإِحْرَامِ وَ عَقْدِهِ وَ الِاشْتِرَاطِ فِيهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 80 – The Prayer of the Ihraam, and tying it, and the stipulations regarding it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/261)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/261)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الَّذِي يَقُولُ حُلَّنِي حَيْثُ حَبَسْتَنِي قَالَ هُوَ حِلٌّ حَيْثُ حَبَسَهُ قَالَ أَوْ لَمْ يَقُلْ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Ibn Bukeyr, from Hamza Bin Humran who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the one who is saying, ‘Free me whereas You<sup>azwj</sup> withheld me’ (the obligations of Hajj). He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Allah<sup>azwj</sup> has Freed him where he was withheld’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Or should he not say it’.
en.sarwar: 6. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Faddal from ibn Bukayr from Hamzah ibn Humran who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who says, ‘Release me as You have withheld me.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘He has released him as He had withheld him.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Should he not say it?”
